project: Observe Insurance – Voice AI Claims Support Agent
phase: Phase 2 – Custom LLM (bring-your-own-brain via VAPI /chat/completions SSE)
premise: Phase 1 (VAPI-native) is tested + working and stays as the reliable demo fallback. Phase 2 swaps ONLY the brain: our server implements an OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions SSE endpoint that VAPI calls each turn. VAPI still owns telephony/STT/TTS/endpointing/barge-in. We own the agentic layer: orchestration, state, tool-calling, extraction, post-processing, CRM, memory.
status: V1 COMPLETE + VALIDATED ON A REAL VAPI TELEPHONY CALL (turn 30). Full agentic layer working end-to-end: VAPI custom-LLM -> our /chat/completions brain (Gemini 2.5 Flash via LiteLLM) -> tools (lookup_claim/confirm_identity/faq_lookup) -> identity gate -> SSE -> end-of-call webhook -> Airtable + SQLite persist -> metrics. All automated tests green (harness deterministic + harness --live + full HTTP E2E). NEXT: V2 work (top priority = streaming-with-tool-interception for latency) + polish; optionally seed more calls for richer metrics/ROI numbers.

live_verified_turn26.result: PASS — 3 real Gemini turns; renderer-as-gate held (no claim_status before verify); status revealed same-turn as confirmation after the confirm_identity fix.
bugs_caught_by_live_run: litellm acompletion lazily imports its MCP handler -> needs orjson (not auto-installed). Would break EVERY server turn. FIX: pip install orjson + added to requirements.txt.
harness live path never called store.init_db() (only app.main does, which harness doesn't import) -> 'no such table: turns'. FIX: store.init_db() at top of live_e2e.
live_verified_turn26.confirm_identity_improvement: confirm_identity(confirmed=True) now returns claim_status IN its tool result (only reachable when confirmed=True, same gate). Without it the reveal slipped to the next turn because the per-turn context is built pre-flip. Renderer-as-gate still governs all subsequent turns.
live_verified_turn26.reorg: docs+data json moved to docs/ (TASK, VAPI_ARCHITECTURE_DOC, SETUP, METRICS, progress*, handoffs, faqs.json, fallbacks.json). knowledge.py loads faqs/fallbacks from docs/. KEPT AT ROOT: CLAUDE.md (Claude Code project-instructions convention), skills-lock.json (tooling lockfile).

webhook_error_handling_turn27.context: Iterated the end-of-call webhook error handling with the user. Two CRM error moments, two behaviors — do NOT conflate them.
webhook_error_handling_turn27.mid_call_crm_error: Happens in /chat/completions (lookup_claim tool). ALREADY BUILT in app/agent/loop.py + docs/fallbacks.json: ToolError(crm_lookup_error) -> turn returns 200 SSE speaking the FIRST-tier mapped fallback ('trouble pulling up your account, confirm your number'); caller responds -> next turn re-runs lookup_claim; 2nd failure (error_counts>=2) -> REPEAT tier -> escalate to human callback + phase=escalating. NO blind retry; the re-attempt is driven by the caller's next utterance. This is exactly the behavior the user described.
webhook_error_handling_turn27.end_of_call_airtable_write: Fires AFTER the call — no customer to re-prompt. FINAL DESIGN (user chose '200 + SQLite fallback, no retry'): attempt Airtable write SYNCHRONOUSLY so we KNOW the outcome; on failure do NOT return 5xx (no blind VAPI retry) — log it, keep the record in SQLite flagged airtable_synced=0, return 200. Airtable success -> airtable_synced=1. Response body: {status:logged, phase, airtable_synced:bool}.
webhook_error_handling_turn27.why_not_5xx: A blind VAPI retry re-hits our endpoint without telling anyone anything and risks duplicate writes. User: 'we dont want retry without telling customer anything.' At end-of-call there IS no customer, so the fallback is durable local persistence + a V2 reconciliation job, not a retry.
webhook_error_handling_turn27.reliability_tiers: Airtable Interactions = CRM source of truth (sync, outcome known, surfaced via airtable_synced). SQLite = durable local copy / fallback (best-effort; its own failure is tolerable and never surfaced -> still 200). 'sqlite is fine' per user.
webhook_error_handling_turn27.schema_change: Added calls.airtable_synced INTEGER (store.py CREATE TABLE). Reconciliation query: SELECT * FROM calls WHERE airtable_synced=0. NOTE: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS won't add the column to a pre-existing DB — the dev DB is ephemeral/gitignored so recreate it; a real migration (ALTER TABLE) is V2.
webhook_error_handling_turn27.tradeoff_accepted: Webhook is now SYNCHRONOUS (post-processing LLM + Airtable write before the 200) — no longer the 'fast async ack' of the original requirement #5. Necessary to know the Airtable outcome. Safe: VAPI webhook timeout ~20s vs our ~2-4s.
webhook_error_handling_turn27.verified: Offline TestClient run: airtable success->200 synced=1; airtable fail->200 NO retry, SQLite row kept synced=0 & findable; sqlite fail->still 200; method/auth/event-filter all pass. harness.py deterministic safety checks still PASS (loop/gate untouched). BackgroundTasks/502 approach from an earlier iteration was REVERTED.
webhook_error_handling_turn27.not_built_v2: Reconciliation job that re-pushes airtable_synced=0 rows to Airtable.

auth_fix_turn28.symptom: First live call: POST /vapi/chat/completions -> 401 every turn; assistant produced nothing; call ended 'Assistant Did Not Receive Customer Audio'. Webhook (/webhooks/vapi/end-of-call) authed fine (200) and end-of-call fell to phase=1 (no in-memory state was ever created because every /chat/completions turn 401'd).
auth_fix_turn28.root_cause: VAPI authenticates differently per surface: server webhooks/tools send `X-Vapi-Secret` header; custom LLM sends the secret in the `Authorization` header. Our verify_vapi_secret only checked X-Vapi-Secret, so the custom-LLM turns 401'd.
auth_fix_turn28.authorization_format_CONFIRMED: Via user pdb inspection of the live request: for the custom LLM, VAPI sends the secret RAW in Authorization (just the token, NO 'Bearer ' prefix). Initial fix assumed 'Bearer <secret>' and stripped 7 chars -> still 401. CORRECTED: auth_token = strip 'Bearer ' IF present else use Authorization raw; accept VAPI_SECRET from x_vapi_secret OR auth_token.
auth_fix_turn28.fix: app/api/dependencies.py verify_vapi_secret accepts VAPI_SECRET from X-Vapi-Secret OR Authorization (raw OR Bearer-prefixed). Empty-secret guarded. Improved rejection log: presence of each header + bearer_prefixed flag (never logs the secret). Verified via TestClient: raw-token->200, Bearer->200, X-Vapi-Secret->200, wrong/missing->401.
auth_fix_turn28.user_config_needed: In VAPI dashboard, set the Custom LLM credential's key/secret to VAPI_SECRET. VAPI forwards it in Authorization (raw). Webhook still uses X-Vapi-Secret = server.secret = same VAPI_SECRET.
auth_fix_turn28.RESOLUTION_query_param: Confirmed via masked fingerprint log that VAPI does NOT forward our secret to a custom LLM — it sends a fixed placeholder Bearer token (fingerprint 'no-…ded(len26)', i.e. no-custom-llm-key-...), NOT settable. FINAL FIX (user chose query-param bypass): Custom LLM URL = {BASE}/vapi/chat/completions?secret=<VAPI_SECRET>. verify_vapi_secret now also reads a `secret` Query param (fastapi Query) and accepts VAPI_SECRET from query OR Authorization(raw/Bearer) OR X-Vapi-Secret. Verified: ?secret=correct->200 (chat endpoint ran the loop end-to-end incl. real Gemini calls), ?secret=wrong->401, X-Vapi-Secret webhook->200. Debug print removed. Tradeoff noted: URL secret can appear in access logs (fine for demo). Webhook keeps X-Vapi-Secret (VAPI sends server.secret for server messages).

live_vapi_call_turn30.milestone: USER PLACED A REAL VAPI TELEPHONY CALL — full end-to-end worked. Phase 2 V1 is validated in production-like conditions (real telephony/STT/TTS + real caller number + our custom-LLM brain).
live_vapi_call_turn30.path_exercised: VAPI custom LLM (Authorization Bearer placeholder ignored; auth via ?secret= query param, VAPI's appended /chat/completions stripped server-side) -> /vapi/chat/completions 200 per turn -> our Gemini tool loop -> SSE -> VAPI TTS; end-of-call webhook -> phase 2 -> Airtable write + SQLite persist.
pre_flight_tests_all_green: harness.py deterministic (identity gate, confirm flip, 2nd-denial escalation, no-record/crm-error/faq fallbacks, SSE shape)

full HTTP E2E (scratchpad/verify_e2e.py): query-param auth in VAPI's exact polluted form, 3-turn SSE over HTTP, tools, gate, end-of-call phase-2 persist (calls+3 turns+8 messages), metrics -> containment 1.0, ~3100 tokens, ~$0.0012/call, tool_success 1.0, ~2.6s/turn
known_v1_characteristics: ~2.6s/turn latency (real Gemini + resolve-then-stream). V2 TOP PRIORITY = streaming-with-tool-interception to cut it.
benign litellm warning 'coroutine VertexLLM.async_completion was never awaited' from the fire-and-forget extractor; no functional impact.
caller name/claim only resolve when VAPI sends call.customer.number (real PSTN call). Web-widget test calls have no number -> name=Unknown / no CRM match (expected).

logging_improvement_turn29.problem: Per-call log files showed only INFO trace, no errors. Root cause: PerCallFileHandler only writes when call_id_var is set, but auth rejections (the 401 on /chat/completions) are logged in the dependency BEFORE call_id is set -> went to console only, never to any file. No durable catch-all log; unhandled exceptions not guaranteed a traceback line.
fix: logging_setup.py: added log/app.log RotatingFileHandler (5MB x3) as a durable CATCH-ALL — every record from every module lands here regardless of call context, incl. pre-call-id errors + full tracebacks. Format now includes %(funcName)s so error lines say where they came from. PerCallFileHandler.emit wrapped in try/except (logging never crashes a request). uvicorn/uvicorn.error routed through our sinks (propagate=True).
main.py: global @app.exception_handler(Exception) -> logger.exception('Unhandled error on <method> <path>') + clean 500 JSON, so uncaught errors always get a traceback+context line.
logging_improvement_turn29.verified: app.log + per-call file both created and both contain the ValueError traceback; auth-style warning logged with no call_id appears in app.log but NOT in any per-call file (correct). harness deterministic PASS; app imports OK.
logging_improvement_turn29.suggested_next: Optionally enrich the auth-rejection warning in dependencies.py to log which credential was present (x_vapi_secret vs bearer, presence/length only — never the secret) — left to user (file was open/being edited).

files_created: app/agent/__init__.py, knowledge.py (faqs+fallbacks loaders), state.py (ConversationState+in-mem store), prompts.py (renderer-as-gate), tools.py (3 tools+ToolError), loop.py (orchestration+extractor+S3 trace)
app/services/store.py (SQLite S2/S3), app/services/postprocess.py (S4 flat structured output, SYNC litellm.completion), app/metrics.py (S4 SQL)
fallbacks.json (7 scenarios x first/repeat), METRICS.md (ROI + tuning loop), harness.py (S1.7)
files_edited: app/api/routers/webhooks.py (S4-D5 phase-2 branch; phase-1 path preserved verbatim)
build_verified_facts: litellm 1.92.0 installed; model id 'gemini/gemini-2.5-flash' recognized (in model_cost); token_counter works offline
LiteLLM Gemini tool-calling = standard OpenAI shape (message.tool_calls[].function.name/.arguments(JSON str)/.id; results back as {role:tool,tool_call_id,content})

DECISION CHANGE from plan: stream final answer by CHUNKING the resolved string (single generation/turn), NOT stream_options include_usage — Gemini has a known content:None bug when include_usage is on. Completion tokens via litellm.token_counter (the planned fallback becomes the primary). Prompt tokens from resp.usage on the no-tool call, token_counter fallback. Real token-streaming w/ tool interception stays V2 (user #1).
postprocess is SYNC (litellm.completion) since the end-of-call webhook is sync def — avoids event-loop juggling; matches S1.4 'inline/synchronous in webhook'
still_pending_live_verify: python harness.py --live (real Gemini tool-calling E2E: greet->lookup->confirm->gated status) — BLOCKED on GEMINI_API_KEY
VAPI end-of-call transcript field path (used msg.transcript || msg.artifact.transcript defensively; confirm on a real report)

versioning_approach.principle: Ship a working end-to-end slice FIRST, then layer depth. Applies to ALL 4 sections.
versioning_approach.V1: Minimal necessary: happy path + main test case (greet -> auth -> confirm identity -> claim status -> end -> write interaction) + 1-2 edge cases, testable end-to-end.
versioning_approach.V2: All edge cases, richer state/context, cross-call memory, full fallback map, FAQ-as-tool, full metrics, complete test cases.
versioning_approach.rule: Every V2 idea gets parked in this schema (per-field / per-feature version tag) so nothing is lost; we decide V2 details later but discuss now.

cross_cutting_decisions.why: Latency for voice, cheap, google-generativeai installed
cross_cutting_decisions.todo: STRICT: web-verify exact model ID + SSE/tool-calling API at build time, do not assume

cross_cutting_decisions.why: Recognize returning callers + recall prior interactions; strongest demo. Source = Airtable Interactions history keyed by caller

cross_cutting_decisions.decision: Post-processing = our own LLM at call end (summary+sentiment+extraction), replaces VAPI analysisPlan
cross_cutting_decisions.why: Showcases the agentic layer; full control

cross_cutting_decisions.decision: S4 metrics = writeup + implemented compute (from logs/transcripts)
ONE SQLite DB (stdlib sqlite3, data/conversations.db) is the shared substrate for S2 (calls, messages), S3 (turns trace) and S4 (metrics-as-SQL). No new dependency.
cross_cutting_decisions.why: Queryable, cross-call memory + trace + metrics from one place; ponytail single-file/per-op-connection/WAL, single-worker ceiling

cross_cutting_decisions.decision: Cross-call memory READ-side is V1 (recognition tier pre-verification, recall tier gated behind identity_verified). claim_status never enters memory.
cross_cutting_decisions.why: Delivers P2-D2 headline demo in V1 while preserving the identity invariant

cross_cutting_decisions.decision: Metrics compute is V1 (app/metrics.py, pure SQL over the shared SQLite DB). Containment uses code-derived escalated, never the LLM.
cross_cutting_decisions.why: Substrate already exists (S2/S3); real numbers for the panel ROI story at near-zero build cost

S1_agent_design.title: Agent Design & Conversation
S1_agent_design.scope: Orchestration loop, system prompt, dialogue states, conversation-flow control, context management, the agent harness, error handling + fallback responses per scenario (no-record, denied identity, alt-verification, escalation, 911 safety, off-topic).
S1_agent_design.status: PLANNING COMPLETE — all 7 subsections decided; ready to build V1

S1_SUMMARY.architecture: Our FastAPI server exposes POST /chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible) that VAPI calls each turn; inside we call Gemini 2.5 Flash via LiteLLM (no LangGraph/LangChain). We OWN the system prompt (strip VAPI's, inject ours). VAPI keeps telephony/STT/TTS/barge-in and only consumes our streamed SSE. Reuse Phase 1 /webhooks/vapi/end-of-call for call-end persist + post-processing.
S1_SUMMARY.two_agents: Responder (streams reply) + parallel extractor (asyncio task, updates current_intent for next turn, lag-by-one accepted).
S1_SUMMARY.state: Pydantic ConversationState in an in-memory dict keyed by call.id (single-worker ceiling; Redis/SQLite=V2). Code-owned V1 fields: call_id, phase(derived), caller_phone, identity_verified, customer, turn_count, error_counts. Extractor-owned V1: current_intent (minimal). Rest of soft state = V2.
S1_SUMMARY.context_engineering: 3 layers merged into ONE system message per turn: [static system prompt]+[dynamic state block], then VAPI-resent user/assistant turns. RENDERER-AS-GATE: claim_status withheld from context until identity_verified=true.
S1_SUMMARY.flow_gates: Two orthogonal axes: phase (code, auth progress) vs current_intent (extractor, soft) -> non-linear handling. Gated: claim data. Open any phase: FAQ/safety. Identity flip via confirm_identity(confirmed) tool (deterministic). V1 deny/no-record=single re-ask then human-followup; alt-verification=V2.
S1_SUMMARY.tools_v1: lookup_claim (reuse crm, returns NAME ONLY), confirm_identity(bool), faq_lookup(query_type=intent enum from faqs.json). Escalation/safety served via faq_lookup KB answers + 1-line prompt safety directive. No escalation tool in V1.
S1_SUMMARY.fallbacks: fallbacks.json, key->{first,repeat} tiers. state.error_counts tracks repeats; 2nd hit of same error escalates to human/callback with warmer/humbler tone. 7 V1 scenarios: crm_lookup_error, api_failure, timeout, llm_error, no_record, identity_denied, malformed_request.
S1_SUMMARY.harness: Local multi-turn sim of VAPI /chat/completions. Deterministic no-LLM gate checks + one real-Gemini E2E happy path. V1 acceptance test.
v1_build_order: 1. faqs loader + fallbacks.json + ConversationState (Pydantic) + in-memory store
3. tool registry: lookup_claim(reuse crm), confirm_identity, faq_lookup
4. POST /chat/completions: build messages -> LiteLLM Gemini tool loop (resolve-then-stream) -> SSE; parallel extractor for current_intent
5. extend end-of-call webhook: persist transcript (S2) + our-own summary/sentiment (S4)
7. harness: deterministic gate checks + real-Gemini E2E happy path
build_time_verifications_STRICT: LiteLLM Gemini 2.5 Flash exact model id + streaming + tool-calling API (web-verify, don't assume)
VAPI incoming messages exact shape: filter role:system defensively, don't assume messages[0]
VAPI end-of-call-report transcript field path (reuse Phase 1 knowledge)

decisions.decision: Framework = LiteLLM only (NO LangGraph/LangChain). Our server is OpenAI-compatible outward (to VAPI); LiteLLM client inward (to Gemini)
decisions.todo: STRICT: web-verify LiteLLM Gemini streaming + tool-calling API at build

decisions.decision: Two agents: main responder (streams the reply) + parallel extraction agent (maintains live ConversationState)
decisions.why: Extraction off the token-streaming path; state stays fresh without blocking latency

decisions.decision: State = Pydantic ConversationState, updated live by extractor; persisted to DB at call end (handled in S2/S4)
Prompt architecture = 3 layers: static system prompt + dynamic state/memory-derived context block + turn history
Non-linear flow: phases greeting/auth -> handling{status|faq|support|escalation} -> end; caller can jump intents. State+gates keep correctness, NOT a rigid FSM
Fallback + error handling externalized to a JSON doc (scenario -> behavior/response)
FAQ knowledge base as JSON, served via tool calling
Extractor IS in V1 but minimal: extracts current_intent only. Richer soft state (topics/pending/unresolved/escalation_reason) = V2
decisions.why: Keeps V1 thin while still exercising the two-agent architecture

decisions.decision: FAQ V1 = tool calling with constrained query_type key (from user-provided faq.json); available keys listed in system prompt; LLM decides whether to call + which key. V2 = RAG/semantic retrieval

Our server OWNS the system prompt (Decision 1). VERIFIED feasible: VAPI custom-LLM is OpenAI-compatible; VAPI only consumes our streamed SSE, so we strip its incoming system message and inject our own static+dynamic prompt before the Gemini call. Config: dashboard Model->Custom LLM->URL={BASE}/chat/completions, auth header; VAPI assistant system-prompt field left as ignored placeholder. Build: filter incoming role:system defensively, don't assume messages[0]
S1.2 context = 3 layers merged into ONE system message per turn: [static system prompt] + [dynamic state block], followed by the VAPI-resent user/assistant turns
decisions.why: Single system message avoids provider ambiguity on multiple system messages

decisions.decision: S1.2 RENDERER-AS-GATE: state stores full customer incl. claim_status, but the dynamic-block renderer only exposes claim_status into context once identity_verified=true. Before that, name-only. The model literally cannot leak what isn't in its context

Sync/async model: async FastAPI (async def /chat/completions), ONE concurrent asyncio task (the extractor), everything the caller waits on stays sequential (responder tool loop; S4 post-processing inline in webhook). Blocking requests/sqlite accepted in the async handler at single-caller demo scale (ponytail); to_thread is V2. Use litellm.acompletion.
decisions.why: Concurrency only where it pays (extractor off the streaming path); avoids premature to_thread plumbing for a single-caller demo. See S1.4.sync_async

S1.2_context_engineering.note: 3 layers merged into one system message; renderer-as-gate withholds claim_status until verified; extractor has its own minimal prompt (current_intent only)

S1.3_flow_gates.note: Two orthogonal axes: phase (code-derived, auth progress) vs current_intent (extractor, soft). Non-linear handling falls out. Gated: claim data (needs identity_verified). Open in any phase: FAQ/general help/safety. Identity flip via confirm_identity(confirmed:bool) tool -> code flips -> renderer exposes claim_status next turn. V1 deny/no-record = single re-ask then 'human will follow up' + log; alt-verification (name+claim_id) + verification_attempts = V2

entry_points.call_end: POST /webhooks/vapi/end-of-call (REUSE Phase 1) -> persist transcript (S2) + run post-processing summary/sentiment (S4) from VAPI-provided transcript

S1.4_orchestration.state_store_v1: In-memory dict keyed by call.id, each value a Pydantic ConversationState. Single-worker ceiling (ponytail). Chosen over stateless-reconstruct because cross-turn tool-result resending by VAPI is undocumented/quirky.
S1.4_orchestration.per_turn_flow: extract call.id+customer.number -> get/create state -> build messages (strip VAPI system, prepend our static+dynamic block, keep turns) -> responder tool loop (lookup_claim/confirm_identity/faq_lookup) -> stream final SSE -> fire extractor in parallel (asyncio task) updating current_intent for next turn (lag-by-one accepted)
S1.4_orchestration.tool_streaming_v1: resolve-then-stream: resolve tool calls with non-streamed model calls, then stream the final answer. Simple; small latency only when a tool fires.

sync_async.model: Async event loop, ONE concurrent background task, everything the caller directly waits on stays sequential.
async: /chat/completions handler = async def (must be, to fire the extractor task + stream SSE)
extractor = asyncio task, fire-and-forget, updates current_intent for the NEXT turn (lag-by-one)
turn-trace SQLite write deferred until after SSE stream closes (off latency path)
sync: responder tool loop within a turn = sequential (resolve-then-stream; caller is waiting, nothing to parallelize; parallelizing = V2_TOP_PRIORITY)
S4 post-processing = inline/synchronous in end-of-call webhook (this is what lets us drop Phase 1 GET /call/{id} polling)
sync_async.blocking_call_gotcha: crm.py uses blocking `requests`; sqlite3 is blocking. In an async handler these block the event loop. V1 DECISION: `# ponytail: blocking requests/sqlite in async handler; fine at single-caller demo scale`. Wrap in asyncio.to_thread ONLY if/when concurrency matters (V2). LiteLLM: use acompletion in the async handler.

S1.4_orchestration.V2_TOP_PRIORITY: Tool-calling + streaming optimization (stream-with-tool-interception, lower latency). User flagged this as the MOST IMPORTANT V2 work.
evidence: VAPI resends conversation history each turn (confirmed)
cross-turn tool-result persistence undocumented -> use own store

v1_tools.lookup_claim: param phone_number (default call.customer.number); crm.lookup_by_phone -> state.customer; RETURNS NAME ONLY (never claim_status)
v1_tools.confirm_identity: param confirmed:bool; true -> flip identity_verified + phase HANDLING; false -> V1 human-followup path
v1_tools.faq_lookup: param query_type = intent enum from faqs.json; returns knowledge_base.faqs[intent].answer

S1.5_tools_faq.faqs_json: Structure: knowledge_base{company_name, support_email, faqs:[{category, intent, questions[], answer}]}. query_type enum = the 8 intents: office_hours, mailing_address, start_new_claim, general_claims_process, submit_documentation, human_representative, emergency_911, out_of_scope. Loader maps intent->answer. questions[] = example phrasings for the system prompt (V2: embeddings/RAG).
S1.5_tools_faq.escalation_safety: Served via faq_lookup (KB owns human_representative/emergency_911/out_of_scope answers). PLUS a one-line safety directive kept in system prompt as belt-and-suspenders so 911 wording is right even if the tool isn't called.
S1.5_tools_faq.note: No separate escalation tool in V1; V2 adds schedule_callback + escalation_reason.

S1.6_fallback_error.approach: Externalize to fallbacks.json (key -> {first, repeat} tiers). Handler tracks state.error_counts[key]; 1st hit -> first tier (retry/standard), 2nd hit of SAME error -> repeat tier (escalate to human/offer_callback, warmer+humbler+gentle-sales tone). Returned as a normal assistant SSE message, never a raw 500.
S1.6_fallback_error.repeat_tone: friendly, humble, apologetic, gentle-sales: e.g. 'I'm so sorry for the hold-up — rather than keep you waiting I'll have one of our representatives call you back personally. We really appreciate your patience.'
S1.6_fallback_error.taxonomy: crm_lookup_error=Airtable-specific; api_failure=model/transport-level; timeout=time budget; llm_error=bad content
S1.6_fallback_error.v2: alt-verification branch, retry-with-backoff, verification_attempts exhaustion, configurable threshold (>2), per-error escalation reasons

subsections.S1.1: State design — Pydantic ConversationState (fields, ownership: code-set vs extractor-set)
subsections.S1.2: Context engineering — 3-layer prompts (system / dynamic state+memory / turn) + how dynamic block is assembled
subsections.S1.3: Flow & gates — phases, non-linear intent routing, identity gate before claim reveal
subsections.S1.4: Orchestration — LiteLLM loop: responder + parallel extractor, streaming, tool loop
subsections.S1.5: Tools & FAQ KB — lookup_claim (reuse crm), faq.json lookup, escalation/transfer
subsections.S1.6: Fallback & error handling — the JSON map (scenario -> response/behavior, error tiers)
subsections.S1.7: Harness/testing — local simulation of VAPI /chat/completions to test the flow

S1.1_state_design.ownership: identity_verified + phase are code-owned (deterministic, zero-lag, protect the gate); soft fields are extractor-owned (lag-tolerant)

identity_verified.note: flipped on caller confirmation; gates claim reveal

error_counts.note: dict[error_type -> count]; drives 2-tier fallback: 1st hit retry/standard, 2nd hit of SAME error -> escalate to human/callback with warmer message

current_intent.note: MINIMAL V1 extractor field — feeds FAQ routing + which query_type to call; extractor runs but only this in V1

verification_attempts.note: added per user; drives alt-verification fallback

S1_agent_design.v1_scope: Responder agent + minimal parallel extractor (current_intent only) + deterministic state + identity gate. FAQ via tool calling with query_type keys (keys in system prompt). Safety/911/escalation scripted in system prompt.
S1_agent_design.v2_scope: Full soft state, non-linear intent routing, cross-call memory injection, full fallback JSON map, FAQ RAG, verification_attempts/escalation_reason logic.

S1.7_harness.approach: Local script simulates VAPI multi-turn POSTs to /chat/completions (no telephony). Two layers: (1) deterministic no-LLM checks on safety-critical logic (renderer gate withholds claim_status until verified, confirm_identity flip, error_counts 2nd-hit escalation, phase derivation); (2) one real-Gemini E2E happy path: greet->phone->lookup_claim->confirm->status->faq_lookup->end-of-call webhook asserts interaction row + summary/sentiment.
S1.7_harness.role: V1 acceptance test (CLAUDE.md goal-driven). S1 not done until this passes.

next_steps: User choice: plan S2-S4 first, or start building S1 V1 now

S2_crm_memory.title: CRM Integration (Airtable) & Memory
S2_crm_memory.scope: Airtable read (lookup_by_phone) + write (interactions), storing the conversation, memory management (in-turn state, persistent transcript, cross-call memory).
S2_crm_memory.status: PLANNING COMPLETE — deep-planned; ready to build V1 (after S1 orchestration loop exists)
S2_crm_memory.reuse: crm.py lookup_by_phone/write_interaction reused verbatim from Phase 1.

S2_SUMMARY.store: ONE SQLite DB (stdlib sqlite3, no new dep) at data/conversations.db serves BOTH S2 (calls+messages) and S3 (turns). Chosen over JSONL/Airtable-table for queryability, cross-call-memory reads, and S4-metrics-as-SQL.
S2_SUMMARY.store_division: Airtable Interactions = business/CRM record (name, summary, sentiment, timestamp), reused verbatim, panel-visible. SQLite = transcript + per-turn trace + cross-call-memory substrate. Both written at call end; turns written per-turn during the call (S3).
S2_SUMMARY.transcript: Belt-and-suspenders: (a) vapi_transcript string on the calls row = authoritative spoken record from VAPI end-of-call-report; (b) messages rows = our structured history (roles + tool calls) = what the model actually processed.
S2_SUMMARY.memory_v1: Cross-call memory READ-SIDE pulled into V1 (user decision). At call start, recent_calls_by_phone(digits) queries SQLite; a memory block is injected into the S1.2 dynamic-context renderer.
S2_SUMMARY.memory_gate: Memory block is TWO-TIER, mirroring the identity gate. Recognition tier (name + prior-call count + last-call date) available pre-verification. Recall tier (prior-call summaries) exposed ONLY after identity_verified=true. NEVER claim_status. Renderer-as-gate [[S1-D12]] governs both claim_status and memory recall — prevents a phone-spoofed caller from hearing prior claim details.
S2_SUMMARY.persist_path: Extend /webhooks/vapi/end-of-call (Phase 1 route, prefix /webhooks): read in-memory ConversationState by call.id -> run S4 summary/sentiment -> write Airtable Interaction (reused) -> write SQLite calls + messages rows.

decisions.decision: Unified SQLite DB at data/conversations.db (stdlib sqlite3, WAL mode, short-lived per-operation connection like the per-line log handler). Serves S2 (calls, messages) AND S3 (turns) AND S4 (metrics-as-SQL).
decisions.why: Queryable, zero new dependency, one substrate for transcript+memory+trace+metrics
decisions.ceiling: ponytail: single DB file + per-op connection, single-worker. Move to pooled connection / Postgres if concurrency grows.

decisions.decision: Two stores with distinct purposes, both written at call end: Airtable Interactions = CRM-visible business record (reused verbatim); SQLite = engineering transcript + trace + memory substrate.
decisions.why: Don't overload Airtable with engineering data; don't lose the panel-visible CRM write

decisions.decision: Persist transcript two ways: vapi_transcript (spoken record, from end-of-call-report) on calls row + structured messages rows (what the model processed, incl. tool calls).
decisions.todo: STRICT: web-verify VAPI end-of-call-report transcript field path at build (artifact.transcript vs message.transcript vs artifact.messages)

decisions.decision: Cross-call memory READ-side is V1 (pulled forward per user). recent_calls_by_phone(digits, limit) at call start -> memory block into S1.2 renderer.
decisions.why: P2-D2 headline demo (recognize returning callers) delivered in V1

decisions.decision: Memory block is two-tier and gated: recognition (name+count+date) pre-verification; recall (prior summaries) only after identity_verified=true; claim_status NEVER in memory.
decisions.why: Preserves the identity invariant against phone spoofing; consistent with lookup_claim name-only [[S1-D12]]

decisions.decision: Persist entry point = extend Phase 1 /webhooks/vapi/end-of-call (reads in-memory state by call.id).
decisions.why: Reuse; state still resident on same worker at call end (ponytail single-worker ceiling)

sqlite_schema.note: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS on startup (init_db called from main.py). caller_phone_digits = last-10 join key, stored unmasked (local DB, it IS the retrieval key; masking is a LOG concern).
sqlite_schema.calls: call_id TEXT PK, caller_phone_digits TEXT, caller_name TEXT, claim_id TEXT, identity_verified INT, current_intent TEXT, final_phase TEXT, num_turns INT, summary TEXT (S4), sentiment TEXT (S4), vapi_transcript TEXT, started_at TEXT, ended_at TEXT, duration_seconds REAL, escalated INT
sqlite_schema.messages: id INTEGER PK, call_id TEXT FK, turn_index INT, role TEXT, content TEXT, tool_name TEXT NULL, created_at TEXT
sqlite_schema.turns: OWNED BY S3 (see S3.trace_schema) — per-turn trace rows in the same DB

v1_build_order: 1. app/services/store.py (stdlib sqlite3): init_db() + save_call() + save_messages() + save_turn() + recent_calls_by_phone(digits, limit). WAL, per-op connection.
2. Call init_db() on startup in main.py
3. Cross-call memory read: recent_calls_by_phone -> two-tier memory block feeding the S1.2 dynamic renderer (recall tier gated on identity_verified)
4. Extend /webhooks/vapi/end-of-call: after Airtable write, persist calls + messages rows from in-memory state
build_time_verifications_STRICT: VAPI end-of-call-report transcript field path (don't assume; reuse Phase 1 payload knowledge + verify)
In-memory ConversationState is still resident (same worker) when the end-of-call webhook fires; keyed by call.id
S2_crm_memory.v1_scope: Airtable read + write-back reused from Phase 1, PLUS SQLite persist (calls+messages) at call end, PLUS cross-call memory read (recognition + gated recall) injected into context.
S2_crm_memory.v2_scope: Richer memory management (semantic recall across many calls, summary-of-summaries), memory decay/relevance ranking, Redis/Postgres for multi-worker, memory write-side beyond last-call summary.
next_steps: Build after S1 orchestration loop exists (S2 hooks into the per-turn context render + end-of-call webhook)

S3_logging_monitoring.title: Logging, Monitoring & Tracing
S3_logging_monitoring.scope: Monitoring/logging touchpoints where errors + metrics are captured; per-call tracing extended to the custom-LLM loop (turns, tool calls, latencies, token counts, errors).
S3_logging_monitoring.status: PLANNING COMPLETE — deep-planned; ready to build V1 (alongside the S1 loop)
S3_logging_monitoring.reuse: logging_setup.py (PerCallFileHandler + call_id_var + masked phones) reused VERBATIM; extend touchpoints across the LLM loop. No change to logging_setup.py.

S3_SUMMARY.two_channels: Human-readable INFO lines -> reused per-call log/<callId>.log (Phase 1 infra). Machine-readable per-turn trace -> SQLite turns table (same DB as S2) = the substrate S4 metrics compute reads via SQL.
S3_SUMMARY.trace_timing: turn trace row written AFTER the SSE stream closes (background task / post-generator), so it is OFF the user-perceived latency path.
S3_SUMMARY.tokens: LiteLLM usage. resolve-then-stream [[S1-D... orchestration]]: non-streamed tool-resolution calls return usage directly; final streamed answer uses stream_options={'include_usage':True} -> usage on the final chunk; fallback litellm.token_counter(model, messages) if a provider omits it.
S3_SUMMARY.timing: stdlib time.perf_counter via a small helper. NO OpenTelemetry/tracing library in V1 (ponytail).

decisions.decision: Trace sink = SQLite `turns` table in the same data/conversations.db as S2. S4 metrics = SQL queries. Written per turn after streaming (off latency path).
decisions.why: Unifies stores; makes S4 containment/handle-time a query, not a log-parser

decisions.decision: Metric list LOCKED for V1 (see metrics_locked). Per-turn + per-call.
decisions.why: V1 scope already fixed the set; no open metric question remains

decisions.decision: Token counts via LiteLLM usage; resolve-then-stream makes tool-resolution usage free; final stream needs stream_options include_usage; litellm.token_counter fallback.
decisions.todo: STRICT: web-verify Gemini 2.5 Flash + LiteLLM include_usage actually returns usage on the final stream chunk at build (known provider-specific gaps)

decisions.decision: Timing via stdlib time.perf_counter + a tiny span helper. No tracing lib in V1.
decisions.why: ponytail: OTel is V2 dashboard territory, overkill for a demo

decisions.decision: Reuse Phase 1 logging infra verbatim (PerCallFileHandler, call_id_var, mask_phone). Set call_id_var at /chat/completions entry so every LLM-loop log line lands in that call's file.
decisions.why: Zero new logging code; consistent per-call files across both phases

trace_schema.turns: id INTEGER PK, call_id TEXT FK, turn_index INT, turn_latency_ms REAL, llm_latency_ms REAL, prompt_tokens INT, completion_tokens INT, total_tokens INT, tool_calls TEXT (JSON), phase_before TEXT, phase_after TEXT, current_intent TEXT, identity_verified INT, error_type TEXT NULL, created_at TEXT

touchpoints_extended: /chat/completions entry: set call_id_var(call.id); INFO turn start (masked phone, turn_index)
each LLM call: INFO latency_ms + token usage
each tool call: INFO name/status/latency (mask phone args via crm.mask_phone)
errors: map to S1.6 fallback taxonomy; logger.exception; error_type recorded on the turn row
extractor task: INFO current_intent extracted (non-blocking, its own line)
build_time_verifications_STRICT: LiteLLM + Gemini 2.5 Flash: include_usage returns usage on final stream chunk (else use token_counter fallback)
FastAPI StreamingResponse: run the turn-trace write after generator close / as a background task so it stays off the perceived-latency path
S3_logging_monitoring.v1_scope: Per-turn latency, token counts, tool-call success/fail + latency, LLM errors (S1.6 taxonomy), phase transitions, current_intent — logged to reused per-call files AND persisted to the SQLite turns table for S4.
S3_logging_monitoring.v2_scope: Aggregation/dashboards, latency percentiles, per-call cost tracking (litellm cost), OpenTelemetry spans, richer trace visualization, alerting.
next_steps: Build alongside the S1 loop: add span timing + turn-trace write inside the /chat/completions orchestration; store.save_turn() from S2

S4_metrics_postprocessing.title: Data & Metrics Evaluation + Post-processing
S4_metrics_postprocessing.scope: Post-call processing (our own summary + sentiment + extraction, replacing VAPI analysisPlan), and the metrics/ROI evaluation: which metrics to track and how to feed them back into prompt/logic tuning.
maps_to_task: Deliverable: metrics & ROI; containment, handle time, prompt tuning
S4_metrics_postprocessing.status: PLANNING COMPLETE — deep-planned; ready to build V1 (after S1 loop + S2 store exist)

S4_SUMMARY.postprocess: Our own LiteLLM Gemini pass runs SYNCHRONOUSLY inside the extended /webhooks/vapi/end-of-call (P2-D3). Replaces VAPI analysisPlan AND removes the Phase 1 polling problem (we own the call, no GET /call/{id} wait). ONE flat structured-output call.
S4_SUMMARY.extraction_v1: Flat schema: {summary:str, sentiment:enum[Positive|Neutral|Negative], resolution_status:enum[resolved|escalated|unresolved], primary_topic:str}. Flat to dodge the nested-Pydantic 400 on Gemini. Stored: summary+sentiment -> Airtable write_interaction (reused) + SQLite calls row; resolution_status+primary_topic -> SQLite calls row.
S4_SUMMARY.containment_source: CODE-derived escalated (final_phase=='escalating' OR any error hit S1.6 repeat tier), NOT the LLM. LLM resolution_status is descriptive only — never trust the model for the headline metric.
S4_SUMMARY.phase_branch: Same webhook serves both phases. If in-memory state exists for call.id -> Phase 2 -> run OUR post-processing. Else -> Phase 1 -> keep existing VAPI-analysis behavior. Preserves the Phase 1 fallback untouched.
S4_SUMMARY.metrics_v1: app/metrics.py = pure SQL aggregates over data/conversations.db (no pandas). Prints a report + returns a dict. Pulled into V1 (user decision) since the S2/S3 substrate already holds everything.
S4_SUMMARY.roi: METRICS.md maps metrics -> ROI narrative + the prompt-tuning feedback loop. Framework/skeleton now; real numbers after live calls.

decisions.decision: Post-processing is OUR synchronous LiteLLM Gemini call inside the end-of-call webhook (P2-D3). No VAPI analysisPlan, no GET /call/{id} polling.
decisions.why: We own the transcript + timing; removes the Phase 1 async-analysis timing problem noted in progress.md

decisions.decision: ONE FLAT structured-output pass -> {summary, sentiment, resolution_status, primary_topic}. response_format json_schema + litellm.enable_json_schema_validation; prompt-and-parse fallback on failure -> S1.6 llm_error tier (sentiment->Neutral, summary from transcript tail).
decisions.todo: STRICT: web-verify LiteLLM + gemini-2.5-flash json_schema returns valid output; keep schema FLAT (nested Pydantic 400 caveat)

decisions.decision: Containment metric uses CODE-derived `escalated` on the calls row, not the LLM's resolution_status.
decisions.why: Headline ROI metric must be deterministic, not model-dependent

decisions.decision: Metrics compute is V1 (pulled forward): app/metrics.py, pure sqlite3 SQL aggregates, no pandas.
decisions.why: Substrate (calls+turns) already exists from S2/S3; compute is a thin SQL script (ponytail)

decisions.decision: Same end-of-call webhook branches on whether in-memory state exists for call.id: Phase 2 -> our post-processing; Phase 1 -> existing VAPI-analysis path.
decisions.why: Extend the reused webhook without breaking the Phase 1 fallback demo

decisions.decision: ROI writeup = METRICS.md (panel deliverable): metric->ROI mapping + prompt-tuning feedback loop. Skeleton now, numbers post-call.

from_turns: est_cost_usd = total_tokens * gemini-2.5-flash price (verify price at build)

prompt_tuning_loop.note: How metrics feed back into prompt/logic tuning (the task asks for this).
levers: primary_topic x escalated -> topics that escalate most -> add/fix FAQ answer or prompt guidance
avg_turn_latency by tool (turns.tool_calls) -> slow tool path -> S1.4 V2 stream-with-tool-interception
negative-sentiment transcripts -> manual review -> tone/prompt fixes (S1.6 repeat-tier)
tokens per turn trend -> context-size tuning (renderer trims / memory-block length)

v1_build_order: 1. app/services/postprocess.py: summarize_call(messages) -> flat structured {summary,sentiment,resolution_status,primary_topic} via LiteLLM Gemini; fallback on failure
2. Wire into end-of-call webhook (S4-D5 branch): feed Airtable write_interaction + SQLite calls row
3. app/metrics.py: SQL aggregates over data/conversations.db -> report dict + CLI (python -m app.metrics)
4. METRICS.md: ROI framework + prompt-tuning loop; fill numbers after live calls
build_time_verifications_STRICT: LiteLLM + gemini-2.5-flash response_format json_schema returns valid {summary,sentiment,...} (flat schema, enable_json_schema_validation, fallback path)
gemini-2.5-flash token pricing for est_cost_usd (web-verify current price, don't hardcode from memory)
S4_metrics_postprocessing.v1_scope: Our synchronous post-call LLM pass -> {summary, sentiment, resolution_status, primary_topic} feeding Airtable + SQLite; PLUS app/metrics.py SQL compute; PLUS METRICS.md ROI skeleton.
S4_metrics_postprocessing.v2_scope: Richer entity extraction, per-topic drill-downs, dashboards/visualization, automated prompt-tuning suggestions, cost alerting, CSAT correlation, date-range aggregation + export.
next_steps: Build after S1 loop + S2 store exist (post-processing consumes state; metrics read the SQLite tables S2/S3 populate)

verified_facts: VAPI Custom LLM = our server exposes OpenAI-shaped POST /chat/completions; VAPI sends {model, messages, temperature, stream, tools?} + injected `call` object each turn.
SSE response: `data: {chat.completion.chunk}\n\n`; [DONE] optional. Both streaming and non-streaming accepted.
Caller phone at call.customer.number (nested, E.164). Tools passed in request; tool_calls detected in stream, backend executes + feeds result back.
build_order_proposed: S1 skeleton: /chat/completions echo loop wired to VAPI, prompt + minimal flow (verify VAPI can talk to our brain end-to-end)
S2: tool-calling loop -> reuse crm lookup; write-back reused
S1 depth: full state/flow/fallbacks + context management
S3: extend tracing across the loop (turns/latency/tokens/errors)

session_optimizing_v1_turn31.summary: Prompt/flow fixes + fuzzy CRM matching + alternative verification. Latency approach re-scoped with primary evidence (probes).
shipped: prompts.py: greet+ask-number FIRST, no tool call in greeting (fixes premature lookup_claim on empty VAPI caller-ID that produced 'couldn't find your number' before the caller spoke).
prompts.py + tools.py: identity confirmation now uses the assignment's exact line 'Am I speaking with {first} {last}?'.

crm.py: generic stdlib fuzzy matcher — all_customers() one-scan + match(customers, phone, name). Phone: exact last-10 digits else Levenshtein<=2 (mis-heard/dropped/extra digit). Name: fuzzy token match (edit<=1) for disambiguation + alternative verification. lookup_by_phone left untouched (Phase 1 fallback).

tools.py: lookup_claim rewritten — 1 candidate->confirm; >1->ask full name (disambiguate); 0->ask full name (alt verification via state.name_attempted) then no_record fallback -> human follow-up. Added optional full_name param to schema.

test_fuzzy_match.py: pure self-check (edit distances, exact/fuzzy/ambiguous/name/typo). harness.py: check_fuzzy_lookup added; check_no_record/check_crm_error updated to patch all_customers and assert the new alt-verification flow.
docs/TASK.md replaced with the verbatim assignment brief (authoritative source).

latency_evidence.method: throwaway probes in scratchpad against real Gemini 2.5 Flash via native google-genai SDK (installed google-genai 2.11.0 in venv; NOT yet in requirements.txt).
findings: function_call arrives as ONE complete chunk (no arg-delta streaming) — confirms interceptor can accumulate whole.
AFC (automatic function calling) must be DISABLED and tools passed as function_declarations so we execute our own state-mutating tools.
Streaming alone is a MODEST win; the dominant cost is the number of sequential LLM round-trips (~1s TTFT floor each). Biggest lever = cut round-trips.

TOP recommended optimization: inline the 8-item KB into the system prompt and DROP faq_lookup so FAQ turns go 2 round-trips -> 1 (~1.7s saved/FAQ turn). Awaiting user's tradeoff decision (verbatim answers + tool-trace vs speed).
Then: migrate the hot path (loop.py) to native google-genai streaming + intercept-every-round (fixes LiteLLM<->Gemini tool-arg-delta bug); keep postprocess.py/extractor on litellm (off latency path).

session_optimizing_v1_turn31.verified: python harness.py green (incl. new checks); python test_fuzzy_match.py green; app imports clean.
session_optimizing_v1_turn31.pending: 1) user decision on inline-KB vs keep faq_lookup. 2) native-SDK streaming rewrite of loop.py (loop.py still resolve-then-chunk). 3) add google-genai to requirements.txt when the migration lands. 4) broader scenario testing on a real VAPI call.

escalation_prompt_tuning_turn32.summary: Escalation & Safety (assignment cap #4) tuned PROMPT-ONLY (no new tools/state, per user).
policy_decisions: Human handoff = deflect EXACTLY once (try to contain/help first), then honor insistence; never loop, never refuse an insisting caller.
Human handoff no longer routed through faq_lookup — governed solely by the system-prompt ESCALATION & SAFETY section (also saves a tool round-trip).
changes: prompts.py STATIC_SYSTEM: replaced SAFETY block with fuller ESCALATION & SAFETY block; flow line no longer sends 'speaking to a human' to faq_lookup.
faqs.json human_representative answer softened to deflect-first (was jumping straight to callback/transfer).
escalation_prompt_tuning_turn32.verified_live: scratchpad/scenario_escalation.py against real Gemini: deflect-once + insist->handoff, 911, off-topic all behave correctly. harness.py still green.
escalation_prompt_tuning_turn32.known_caveat: Prompt-only escalation does NOT set phase=ESCALATING, so a deflect->handoff is not counted in the code-derived containment metric (webhooks.py _phase2_record). Add a state hook later if escalations must appear in deliverable-#3 numbers.
